name: SLE - ANA Antibody Titer Query (Positive)
description: Tests ability to find SLE patients via positive ANA (Antinuclear Antibody) titer. ANA is the primary screening lab for SLE — titers ≥1:160 (numerically encoded as 160 in our data) are clinically significant. Synthea generates ANA titers of 160-1280 for SLE patients (positive). The ANA test has high sensitivity (~95%) but lower specificity — many autoimmune conditions can produce positive ANA.
prompt: Find patients with positive antinuclear antibody (ANA) titer lab results at or above 160 — the clinical threshold for autoimmune disease workup.

prompts.broad: Find patients with elevated antinuclear antibody (ANA) titer — values at or above 160 (1:160 dilution), the standard threshold for clinically significant ANA positivity.
prompts.expert: I need Observation resources with elevated ANA titer. Use LOINC code 5048-4 (Nuclear Ab [Titer] in Serum by Immunofluorescence). Filter by value-quantity at or above 160 (the standard 1:160 cutoff). Query: Observation?code=http://loinc.org|5048-4&value-quantity=ge160

metadata.algorithm_path_description: Patients with positive ANA suggesting autoimmune disease. Sensitive but not SLE-specific.

required_codes.display: Nuclear Ab [Titer] in Serum by Immunofluorescence
